title: Demand
description: The machine-readable envelope for the YAML frontmatter every repo's demands/*.md carries (DEMAND_SYSTEM.md §5) — a repo's outbox record of a cross-repo need it must not fix itself. The markdown body beneath the frontmatter stays free-form human detail (What we need / Why / What we do once closed); only the envelope validates against this schema. Field casing follows the convention already live in committed demand files (kebab-case for multi-word fields), not this repo's usual camelCase — this schema formalizes an existing on-disk YAML convention rather than inventing a new one. Demand files written before this schema existed (e.g. PlantPal's first) will not validate; the demand-coordinator's ingest keeps a tolerant fallback for those, per its own spec.

date.description: The date the demand was raised.

from.description: The origin repo's functional name (D002) — same shape as app.manifest's name / hexagon.descriptor's functionalName.

to.description: Target repo(s), functional names. More than one entry means a multi-hexagon demand — the coordinator sequences it into ordered sub-demands.

capability.description: One line: what the origin needs to exist. Precise per-target detail belongs in the markdown body, not here.

acceptance-criteria.description: How the origin will know this is truly done — one entry per criterion. Promoted out of free-form markdown into structured frontmatter so the coordinator (and eventually an AI-triage pass) can check completion mechanically instead of re-parsing prose.

needs-owner.description: True if any part of this demand requires an owner ruling or design decision before any agent can act on it.

status.description: Origin-owned lifecycle field. Deliberately excludes the coordinator-internal states (ingested/evaluated/ready/in-progress/done-claimed/pending-approval) — those live in the coordinator's own ledger, not the origin's file.
